mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in Retail Sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where RSAFS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) over a three-month period signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances where the UNRATE trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of persistently low Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) and rising Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) suggests an accommodative monetary policy falling behind inflationary pressures, leading to asset price inflation and positive S&P 500 returns as investors seek real returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns when FEDFUNDS is low and CPIAUCSL is rising, or the Rank IC is consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: Robust Housing Starts (HOUST) during an economic recovery phase indicate strong underlying demand and investment, boosting market sentiment and leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST values above 500k units during recovery periods do not correlate with positive 5-day SPX returns, or the average return is negative.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in Industrial Production (INDPRO) signifies expanding manufacturing and supply-side capacity, which supports corporate earnings and leads to higher equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth does not lead to positive SPX returns over 60 days in expansionary periods, or the correlation is negative.

mechanism_narrative: A significant monthly decline in Non-Farm Payrolls (PAYEMS) signals a severe weakening of the labor market, leading to widespread economic pessimism, reduced consumption, and negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 100,000 persons month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:payroll changes are key labor market indicators
failure_condition: SPX shows positive or flat returns following significant PAYEMS declines in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of sluggish GDP growth (GDPC1) and low inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates a disinflationary environment with weak aggregate demand, which typically leads to lower corporate revenues and negative equity performance over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 1.5%
evidence_refs: fact:disinflationary pressures hurt corporate pricing power
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 60-day returns when both GDP growth and inflation are low, or the Rank IC is consistently positive.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of extremely low interest rates (FEDFUNDS), any unexpected acceleration in inflation (CPIAUCSL) can trigger fears of future monetary tightening, leading to immediate negative market reactions and S&P 500 declines over a 5-day horizon.
regime_hint: zero lower bound interest rate environment
failure_condition: SPX shows positive or neutral 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL rises unexpectedly in a low FEDFUNDS environment, or the average return is positive.
